title: Mantle upstream topology graph
description: Structural schema for mantle-topology-graph.json. Structural/enum/conditional rules are expressed here; referential and graph-semantic invariants (edge endpoints exist, group nodes have no edges, fork-copy nodes have an out-edge, group.lines resolve, line nodes have edges) are checked by the companion validator and listed under x-graph-invariants.

allOf.comment: A group node must declare lines.
Non-registry edges must carry a machine-readable ref/baseline (not just prose detail).

ref_kind.description: What KIND of provenance anchors this edge. For edges with no resolvable pin (pure fork lineage / whole-repo fork) use 'fork-lineage'/'whole-repo-fork' + provenance, instead of stuffing prose into tag/baseline_tag.

provenance.description: Free-text provenance note for edges whose anchor is not a resolvable ref.

ref.description: Human-readable ref label (prefer a resolvable tag/branch/rev when one exists; otherwise use ref_kind+provenance).

expand_groups.description: If true, participants/affects may reference group nodes (meaning 'all their line nodes'). Default false: constraints should point at line nodes, not edgeless group nodes.

x-graph-invariants: Every edge.from / edge.to resolves to an existing node id.
Group nodes (is_group=true) have NO incident edges; their line nodes carry the edges.
Every id in a group's 'lines' resolves to a node whose member_of points back to that group.
Every line node (member_of set) has at least one incident edge (so it is reachable in traversal).
mantle-fork-copy nodes must have at least one OUT edge (they are consumed by something).
Every constraint participant / fork / affects id resolves to an existing node id.
node.contains[] and node.parent resolve to existing node ids.
Constraints must not reference group nodes in participants/forks/affects unless the constraint sets expand_groups=true (groups are edgeless display-only).

Every non-registry edge (type not in registry/submodule/go-require) carries a machine-readable ref anchor (one of: ref_kind, branch, tag, rev, resolved_sha, merge_base, baseline_tag, subtree_baseline, kernel_version) — prose 'detail' alone is not sufficient.

An edge.via.ref_kind is backed by its concrete field: tag->tag, branch->branch, rev->rev, exact-version->version|ref, merge-base->merge_base, subtree-baseline->subtree_baseline, baseline-tag->baseline_tag, kernel-inherited->kernel_version, fork-lineage|whole-repo-fork->provenance.

Version lines stay separated under traversal: paradigmxyz-reth-v2.2.0 does not reach mantle-kona, paradigmxyz-reth-v1.6.0 does not reach mantle-reth, mantle-revm-elysium does not reach mantle-kona/mantle-op-succinct, mantle-revm-v2.2.2 does not reach mantle-reth.
x-validator-note: JSON Schema enforces the structural + ref_kind-backing rules; the referential, group-edgelessness, line-reachability, fork-copy-out-edge, constraint-not-group, and version-line-separation invariants are enforced by scripts/validate_topology.py. JSON Schema validation ALONE is insufficient — run the validator.
